title: Seed pane inherits daemon CWD, not client cwd — breaks claude --resume
description: A claude (Claude Code) session started inside a phux shell can't be resumed: 'claude --resume' from the project dir finds no history. Root cause: the seed/initial pane never sets a cwd and inherits the daemon's cwd. Claude keys its transcript at HOME/.claude/projects/<cwd-hash>/*.jsonl on the pane's actual cwd (= daemon cwd), so the transcript IS written and flushed, just under the hash of the wrong directory. The session NAME is derived from the client cwd basename, so it looks like it's in the project dir while the pane runs in the daemon dir. HOME/env inherited correctly (not the cause); data not lost, just filed under wrong project hash. Fix: client should send its real cwd (not None) for the initial attach, and resolve_create_if_missing must apply it, mirroring the split path's resolve_inherited_cwd. Likely warrants an ADR. Verified against code: resolve_create_if_missing ignores _cwd (phux-server/src/runtime/attach.rs:159, doc-comment :140-142); client sends cwd:None (phux/src/commands/attach.rs:177); seed_session_with_pty sets no .cwd() (phux-server/src/runtime/commands.rs:63-94); split path DOES honor cwd (attach.rs:496-500). Secondary (lower prio): TerminalSignal::Kill group-SIGKILLs via killpg (terminal_actor/mod.rs:1324-1344), so killing a pane while claude is foreground gives no chance to flush; clean shell-exit reaps gracefully. Reported via phux-mobile bead phux-mobile-3ru. See SEED_CWD_BUG.md.
notes: Implemented on branch claude-persistence. Client (attach.rs:180) now sends real cwd via std::env::current_dir() in the Last->CreateIfMissing fallback; server resolve_create_if_missing (attach.rs) applies wire cwd to the seed pane's CommandBuilder in the PTY branch. Added acceptance test create_if_missing_seeds_pane_in_wire_cwd (spawn_terminal.rs) proving the seed pane runs in the wire cwd. Traced the real path: naked phux -> Last refused on fresh server -> CreateIfMissing{project-name} slow path = the reported name/cwd-mismatch case. Pre-seeded startup session already inherits the spawning client's cwd (fork), so no change needed there. No wire/spec change (cwd field already existed). Treated as bug fix, not ADR (code comment already documented the seed-in-server-CWD as a known follow-up). fmt+clippy clean.

title: ASCII QR code for 'phux pair' token (mobile scan)
description: Companion to the mobile-app QR bead. 'phux pair' (crates/phux/src/commands/pair.rs, ADR-0031) currently mints a bearer pairing token and prints it as plain text alongside the cert fingerprint for a wss:// consumer. The mobile app wants to scan a QR instead of copy/paste. Add an ASCII/Unicode QR rendering of the pairing payload (token + connection URL/fingerprint) printed to the terminal after minting. No QR code exists in the tree today (grep 'qr' finds none). Scope: pick a small qr-code crate (justify per no-new-deps rule), define the exact payload string the mobile app expects (coordinate with the phux-mobile QR bead), render half-block/ASCII QR, and gate behind a flag or make it default output. Ensure it degrades in narrow terminals.

title: Add 'phux detach' CLI command mirroring the ctrl+a d keybinding
description: Today detach exists only as a client-side keybinding action ('detach', bound to ctrl+a d) in crates/phux-client/src/attach/action_registry.rs:248 ('Detach this client from the session'). There is no 'phux detach' subcommand (crates/phux/src/commands/ has attach/kill/etc. but no detach.rs). Add a 'phux detach' CLI verb so a client can be detached from outside the attach UI (e.g. scripting, or detaching a different/stuck client). Needs: new commands/detach.rs, wire into commands/mod.rs and the clap command tree, and a server path to signal the target client to detach (reuse whatever the keybinding action dispatches). Decide targeting: current client vs by session/client id.

title: Pane Kill sends group-SIGKILL, gives foreground claude no chance to flush
description: Secondary/lower-priority companion to phux-0db. TerminalSignal::Kill sends a group-SIGKILL via killpg (phux-server/src/terminal_actor/mod.rs:1324-1344, :1340-1343). Killing a pane from the client while claude is the foreground process (instead of exiting claude first) gives claude no chance to flush its transcript. The clean shell-exit path (handle_pty_eof, mod.rs:1749-1756) reaps gracefully and sends no signal, so 'quit claude, then exit the shell' is fine. Consider a graceful SIGTERM-then-SIGKILL with a grace window for pane kills.
